etymology.description: 糸(糹) (sī, 'silk; thread') + 巠(巠) (jīng, phonetic) -> textile threads metaphorically referring to 'classics' or 'scriptures'
etymology.interpretation: The character 經(jīng, 'classic; sacred book; scripture') combines the idea of silk threads with the phonetic component 巠 (jīng), representing the weaving of ideas and traditions into a coherent and eternal form, akin to weaving fabric.
etymology.simplification: In simplified form, 經 was simplified by replacing 巠(巠) (jīng) with 圣(圣) (shèng), which shares a similar sound.

examples.chinese: 《道德经》是中国古代的经典(《道德經》是中國古代的經典)。
examples.pinyin: Dào Dé Jīng shì Zhōngguó gǔdài de jīngdiǎn.
examples.english: The Dao De Jing is an ancient Chinese classic.

examples.chinese: 他每天读经(他每天讀經)，祈求心灵平和(祈求心靈平和)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān dú jīng, qíqiú xīnlíng pínghé.
examples.english: He reads the scriptures every day, seeking inner peace.

examples.chinese: 佛经包含深厚智慧(佛經包含深厚智慧)。
examples.english: The Buddhist scriptures contain profound wisdom.
